hero.intro: Alex and as a Senior Product Designer

cases.title: Selected cases
cases.description: Three chapters of the same product ecosystem — each with its own audience, mental model and success metric.

items.title: Rizzult — App for Influencers
items.description: Mobile product for creators to manage deals, content and payouts. From discovery to deep dashboards.

items.title: Rizzult Talent Manager
items.description: Web cockpit for talent managers to oversee rosters, negotiate with brands and close contracts.

items.title: Rizzult for Brands
items.description: Self-serve platform for brands to launch campaigns, track performance and pay creators.

about.title: About me
about.body: Product designer with 7+ years shipping B2B and B2C products end-to-end — from problem framing and research to pixel-precise delivery. Happy to own a feature, happier to own an outcome.

process.title: My process
process.description: Five steps from problem to production. Lightweight enough to move fast, structured enough to defend in a product review.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.description: Stakeholder interviews, desk research, competitor teardown.

steps.title: Define
steps.description: Problem statement, user jobs, success metrics agreed in writing.

steps.title: Explore
steps.description: Divergent sketches, low-fi flows, quick usability loops.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: High-fi UI, design-system tokens, interaction specs handed off with zero ambiguity.

steps.title: Ship & learn
steps.description: Paired with engineering during build, post-launch analytics review, next-bet shortlist.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: What I practice daily. Bold if it's on the critical path for most briefs I take.

experience.title: Experience
experience.description: Where I've shipped, studied and learned the hard lessons.

bullets: Owned end-to-end design of three B2B2C products sharing one design system.
Led weekly research loops with creators, agencies and brand ops.
Shipped the 0 → 1 talent-manager cockpit used by 40+ agencies.
Helped pivot a consumer fintech from card swipe to cashback marketplace.
Built the in-house design-system from zero tokens to 200+ components.
Delivered MVP UI for two SaaS startups (one acquired).
Partnered with founders on positioning, IA and investor decks.
Coached two junior designers through their first shipped features.
Visual-identity work for early-stage DTC brands.
Began transitioning from brand to product systems.
Thesis on accessible wayfinding in public transit.

footerCta.title: Let's connect
footerCta.description: Open to product design roles, collaborations, and meaningful conversations.

rizzult-influencers.title: Rizzult — App for Influencers
rizzult-influencers.subtitle: The creator-side mobile app. From empty-state to payouts dashboard.
rizzult-influencers.overview: Transformed Rizzult's influencer workflow from a manual, scout-led process into a mobile self-service platform, allowing creators to independently discover projects, apply to campaigns, and manage collaborations.
rizzult-influencers.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.
appFlow: Review the company and campaign rules

impactOverview.title: Independent creator workflow
impactOverview.body: Enabled influencers to manage key collaboration steps independently in one mobile experience.

impactOverview.title: Better campaign transparency
impactOverview.body: Improved access to brand information, campaign requirements, and participation guidelines before applying.

impactOverview.title: Reduced operational friction
impactOverview.body: Structured the flow from application to content approval and publication confirmation into a clearer process.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: The main challenge was to design the product from the ground up and turn a broad idea into a clear, usable mobile experience for influencers. The app needed to combine two different value areas — creator growth and monetization — in a way that felt simple, relevant, and easy to navigate on mobile.

myRole.title: My Role
myRole.intro: As a Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end product logic and experience strategy.

items.title: UX Strategy
items.body: Mapping complex core product logic and multi-sided marketplace flows.

items.title: Visual Design
items.body: Establishing a high-fidelity visual design language for the mobile-first era.

items.title: Design Systems
items.body: Building and scaling the project through a dedicated design system.

items.title: Prototyping
items.body: Developing complex interactive prototypes for rapid user validation.

process.title: Process
process.intro: Working closely with the Product Manager, Product Owner, and creators to shape, test, and improve the product experience.

items.title: Defining Structure
items.body: Collaborative work with the Product Director to shape app structure and core logic.

items.title: Concepts to Prototypes
items.body: Translating ideas into interface concepts, flows, and interactive prototypes.

items.title: Usability Testing with Creators
items.body: Reviewing and validating the experience with a small group of UGC creators.

items.title: Iterating the Product
items.body: Iterated on product flows and interactions based on testing in Figma and the live product.

keyDecisions.title: Key UX Decisions
keyDecisions.intro: Key UX decisions were focused on reducing friction, improving clarity, and supporting creators throughout the flow.

items.title: Reducing self-service complexity
items.body: Many creators were not used to handling the full campaign flow themselves, since similar processes had previously been supported by scouts. Because of this, I focused on making the experience as simple and guided as possible.

items.title: Making relevant opportunities easy to find
items.body: A key decision was to help influencers quickly identify projects that matched their profile and were worth applying to, reducing the effort required to browse and evaluate options.

items.title: Simplifying the application and submission flow
items.body: The participation flow was designed to feel lightweight and clear, so users could easily apply to a project, complete the required steps, and later submit content for moderation without unnecessary friction.

items.title: Making statuses and progress visible
items.body: Special attention was given to statuses, progress states, and next-step clarity. Users needed to always understand where they were in the process — whether waiting for approval, preparing content, submitting materials, or expecting payment.

items.title: Adapting hierarchy for mobile clarity
items.body: Since the product was mobile-first, I designed the interface to support quick scanning, clear prioritization, and fast actions, especially for repeat tasks.

impact.title: Impact
impact.intro: Early outcomes from launch showed the product's potential to support creators more independently and efficiently.
items: The product was launched and showed early signs that creators could independently find projects, apply to campaigns, and complete publishing-related actions.
The experience helped validate a more self-service model, with the potential to reduce reliance on scouts and lower manual operational effort.
Positive feedback from influencers highlighted the value of the app's educational and utility-driven content.
Features related to content planning helped creators organize their work more effectively and increased the app's practical day-to-day relevance.
These elements supported stronger user loyalty and encouraged more regular engagement with the product.

rizzult-talent-manager.title: Rizzult Talent Manager
rizzult-talent-manager.subtitle: The agency cockpit. Roster management, deal flow, and payouts in one place.
rizzult-talent-manager.overview: Designed a centralised web cockpit for talent agencies to manage creator rosters, negotiate brand deals, and track campaigns — replacing disconnected spreadsheets and email threads with a single integrated workspace.
rizzult-talent-manager.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.

impactOverview.title: Centralised roster control
impactOverview.body: Gave talent managers a single source of truth for creator relationships, stats, and active campaigns.

impactOverview.title: Faster deal pipeline
impactOverview.body: Streamlined the negotiation-to-signature flow, reducing back-and-forth between agencies and brands.

impactOverview.title: Transparent payout management
impactOverview.body: Brought payment tracking into the same workspace, eliminating manual reconciliation steps.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: The core challenge was to design a complex B2B management tool that remained simple enough for daily use. Talent managers were juggling dozens of creators, brands, and active campaigns across disconnected email threads, spreadsheets, and messaging apps. The goal was to bring this fragmented workflow into one cohesive, professional interface.

myRole.title: My Role
myRole.intro: As Lead Product Designer, I drove product strategy, interaction design, and the visual system for the entire cockpit.

items.title: UX Strategy
items.body: Mapping complex agency workflows and multi-party negotiation flows.

items.title: Visual Design
items.body: Designing a dense, data-rich interface that stays readable at a glance.

items.title: Design Systems
items.body: Extending the shared system to cover B2B table and form patterns.

items.title: Prototyping
items.body: Building interactive negotiation flow prototypes for stakeholder validation.

process.title: Process
process.intro: Deep collaboration with agency stakeholders and internal PMs to map real talent management workflows.

items.title: Stakeholder Research
items.body: Shadowing talent managers and mapping their existing tools and mental models.

items.title: Information Architecture
items.body: Structuring roster, deal, and payment data into a navigable hierarchy.

items.title: Prototype Testing
items.body: Validating key flows with agency power users across multiple rounds.

items.title: Iterating on Density
items.body: Refining data-table layouts and filters based on real usage feedback.

keyDecisions.title: Key UX Decisions
keyDecisions.intro: Decisions centred on balancing information density with usability for professional daily use.

items.title: Roster as the primary entry point
items.body: Talent managers think in creators first, campaigns second. Making the roster the home screen aligned the tool with their natural mental model and shortened time-to-task.

items.title: Inline deal status visibility
items.body: Deal stages needed to be visible without opening details. Inline status chips let managers scan pipeline health across many deals at once.

items.title: Separating negotiation from execution
items.body: Pre-signature negotiation and post-signature campaign tracking were distinct enough to warrant separate views, reducing cognitive load at each stage.

items.title: Making payment expectations transparent
items.body: Delayed payment visibility was a major pain point. Bringing expected payout dates and amounts into the campaign view reduced inbound queries from creators.

items.title: Progressive disclosure for complex data
items.body: With many fields per creator and deal, progressive disclosure kept the default view clean while giving power users access to full detail on demand.

impact.title: Impact
impact.intro: The cockpit launched to 40+ agencies and demonstrated measurable improvements in agency efficiency.
items: Agencies reported significantly reduced time spent switching between tools to track creator and deal status.
The deal pipeline view became the primary workspace for brand negotiations, replacing email-based tracking.
Payment transparency features reduced the volume of creator payment enquiries reaching talent managers.
The design system extension laid the foundation for future B2B feature development across the platform.
Positive reception from early agency partners led to faster onboarding of new clients.

rizzult-brands.title: Rizzult for Brands
rizzult-brands.subtitle: Self-serve campaign launcher. Discovery, briefing, payments — in one flow.
rizzult-brands.overview: Designed the brand-facing side of the Rizzult platform — a self-serve campaign tool that lets marketing teams discover relevant creators, define campaign briefs, track content performance, and manage creator payouts without agency intermediaries.
rizzult-brands.nda: Some details are hidden under NDA.

impactOverview.title: Self-serve campaign launch
impactOverview.body: Enabled brands to go from brief to active campaign independently, without requiring agency involvement.

impactOverview.title: Creator discovery clarity
impactOverview.body: Gave brand teams the data they needed to evaluate creator fit before committing to a collaboration.

impactOverview.title: End-to-end payment flow
impactOverview.body: Connected campaign completion to creator payout in one traceable workflow, reducing finance team overhead.

goal.title: Goal
goal.body: Brands needed a way to run influencer campaigns without relying on agencies as intermediaries. The challenge was to design a platform that made creator discovery, campaign briefing, and payment feel approachable for non-specialist marketing teams while handling the complexity behind the scenes.

myRole.title: My Role
myRole.intro: As Lead Product Designer, I owned the end-to-end brand-side experience from discovery through payment.

items.title: UX Strategy
items.body: Mapping brand-side campaign workflows and identifying key drop-off points.

items.title: Visual Design
items.body: Designing a trustworthy, business-grade interface that reflects brand credibility.

items.title: Design Systems
items.body: Adapting shared tokens to support the brand-facing product context.

items.title: Prototyping
items.body: Validating the brief-to-launch funnel with brand marketing stakeholders.

process.title: Process
process.intro: Worked closely with brand-side stakeholders and internal growth teams to understand campaign launch needs.

items.title: Brand Stakeholder Research
items.body: Interviewing marketing managers to understand how they currently run influencer activations.

items.title: Discovery Flow Design
items.body: Designing the creator search and shortlisting experience to match brand evaluation criteria.

items.title: Brief Builder Testing
items.body: Iterating on the campaign brief form with real brand users to reduce completion friction.

items.title: Payment Flow Validation
items.body: Working with finance stakeholders to ensure the payout flow met compliance and transparency requirements.

keyDecisions.title: Key UX Decisions
keyDecisions.intro: Decisions focused on making the platform feel safe and efficient for brand marketers running campaigns independently.

items.title: Guided campaign creation
items.body: Many brand users were running their first influencer campaign. A step-by-step brief builder with contextual guidance reduced uncertainty and increased completion rates.

items.title: Creator fit signals over raw numbers
items.body: Rather than exposing raw follower counts, the discovery interface surfaced relevance signals — audience match, past campaign categories, and engagement quality — to support better decision-making.

items.title: Separation of discovery and campaign management
items.body: Discovery and active campaign management were separated into distinct modes to avoid overwhelming first-time users with the full product surface before they were ready.

items.title: Payment transparency from day one
items.body: Brands needed to know total campaign costs upfront. Showing estimated payout totals during brief creation reduced sticker shock at checkout and improved conversion.

items.title: Performance data at the right granularity
items.body: Campaign performance views were designed for marketing managers, not analysts — summary metrics with clear trend indicators, not raw data tables.

impact.title: Impact
impact.intro: The brand platform validated the self-serve model and opened a new acquisition channel for the business.
items: Brand teams were able to launch campaigns independently without requiring agency or operations support.
The guided brief builder reduced brief completion drop-off and produced higher-quality campaign specifications.
Creator discovery features improved brand confidence in creator selection, reducing post-selection second-guessing.
The integrated payment flow reduced manual finance team involvement in creator payout processing.
Positive early reception supported the business case for continued self-serve product investment.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you're after doesn't exist — or the link is dated. Head back to the homepage and try again.
